patches.hypothesis: Refined the patch to remove the hardcoded value and handle general cases while preserving test-passing behavior and avoiding overfitting.
The bug is caused by the lack of a null check in the method.
patches.original_code: /** * Constructs a new String by decoding the specified array of bytes using the ISO-8859-1 charset. * * @param bytes * The bytes to be decoded into characters, may be null * @return A new String decoded from the specified array of bytes using the ISO-8859-1 charset, or * null if the input byte array was null. * @throws NullPointerException * Thrown if {@link Charsets#ISO_8859_1} is not initialized, which should never happen since it is * required by the Java platform specification. * @since As of 1.7, throws {@link NullPointerException} instead of UnsupportedEncodingException */ public static String newStringIso8859_1(final byte[] bytes) { return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); }

patches.hypothesis: The cause of the bug is the absence of a null check in the method, which leads to a NullPointerException when the input byte array is null.
patches.changes: - return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); + if (bytes == null) { return null; } else { return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); }
patches.fixed_method: public static String newStringIso8859_1(final byte[] bytes) { if (bytes == null) { return null; } else { return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); } }
patches.diff: - return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); + if (bytes == null) { return null; } else { return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); }
patches.original_code: /** * Constructs a new String by decoding the specified array of bytes using the ISO-8859-1 charset. * * @param bytes * The bytes to be decoded into characters, may be null * @return A new String decoded from the specified array of bytes using the ISO-8859-1 charset, or * null if the input byte array was null. * @throws NullPointerException * Thrown if {@link Charsets#ISO_8859_1} is not initialized, which should never happen since it is * required by the Java platform specification. * @since As of 1.7, throws {@link NullPointerException} instead of UnsupportedEncodingException */ public static String newStringIso8859_1(final byte[] bytes) { return new String(bytes, Charsets.ISO_8859_1); }
